answer:
\boxed{B}

Protein has the highest thermic effect of food (TEF), typically around 20–30%, because it requires more energy to digest, absorb, and metabolize compared to carbohydrates (~5–10%) or fats (~0–3%). This is due to the metabolic processes involved in breaking down amino acids and synthesizing new proteins. Carbohydrates and fats have lower TEF values, making protein the most thermogenic macronutrient.
